commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a9cb4e56d62007a56452b3007c778217bbeadec7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a built-in Claude Opus 4.8 pricing entry and regression coverage for pricing and scanning Opus 4.8 usage rows.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main lacks a built-in claude-opus-4-8 entry, and source inspection shows claudeCostUSD returns nil after models.dev lookup misses; I did not run live account probes because AGENTS.md discourages unrequested provider validation.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof, so this should stay in human review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: Add Opus 4.8 to built-in Claude pricing table
target.body:
## Summary
Opus 4.8 token cost displayed as empty (#1210). `claudeCostUSD` tries the
models.dev catalog first, then falls back to the built-in Claude pricing table.
That table had entries through Opus 4.7 but none for `claude-opus-4-8`, so when
the models.dev cache is missing or stale the lookup returns nil and the cost
card renders empty.

This adds a `claude-opus-4-8` entry mirroring the existing Opus 4.5/4.6/4.7
entries: $5 input / $25 output / $6.25 cache-write / $0.50 cache-read per 1M
tokens. Values confirmed against the live models.dev catalog, which lists Opus
4.8 identically to 4.7. `normalizeClaudeModel`'s dated-suffix stripping means a
future `claude-opus-4-8-YYYYMMDD` id resolves to this entry too.

## Tests
- `CostUsagePricingTests."claude cost supports opus48"` — built-in table prices Opus 4.8 to the exact expected value.
- `CostUsageScannerClaudeRegressionTests."claude opus 4 8 issue row gets priced"` — end-to-end: a Claude JSONL row with `claude-opus-4-8` now produces a non-zero cost.

## Verification
`swiftformat Sources Tests --lint` and `swiftlint --strict` both clean. New tests run via `swift test --filter CostUsagePricingTests` / `--filter CostUsageScannerClaudeRegressionTests`.

Fixes #1210
target.author: devyrpauli
